title: quercus bicolor var. lyrata
intro: Quercus bicolor var. lyrata, also known as the River Birch or Paper Birch, is a deciduous tree species native to eastern North America, characterized by its distinctive white or light-gray bark and slender branches. The medicinal properties of Quercus bicolor var. lyrata include its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which are attributed to its high content of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and soothe digestive issues, making it a popular natural remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of Quercus bicolor var. lyrata include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to make tinctures, salves, and other topical applications.
